children.text: Provide for interagency coordination of Federal engineering biology research, development, and other activities undertaken pursuant to the Initiative.
Establish and periodically update goals and priorities for the Initiative.

children.label: A
children.text: guides the activities of the Initiative for purposes of meeting the goals and priorities established under (and updated pursuant to) paragraph (2); and

children.label: i
children.text: the Initiative’s support for long-term funding for interdisciplinary engineering biology research and development;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Initiative’s support for education and public outreach activities;

children.label: I
children.text: an applied biorisk management research plan;

children.label: II
children.text: recommendations for integrating security into biological data access and international reciprocity agreements;

children.label: III
children.text: recommendations for manufacturing restructuring to support engineering biology research, development, and scaling-up initiatives; and

children.label: IV
children.text: an evaluation of existing biosecurity governance policies, guidance, and directives for the purposes of creating an adaptable, evidence-based framework to respond to emerging biosecurity challenges created by advances in engineering biology;

children.label: iv
children.text: how the Initiative will contribute to moving results out of the laboratory and into application for the benefit of society and United States competitiveness; and

children.label: v
children.text: how the Initiative will measure and track the contributions of engineering biology to United States economic growth and other societal indicators.

Develop a national genomic sequencing strategy to ensure engineering biology research fully leverages plant, animal, and microbe biodiversity, as appropriate and in a manner that does not compromise economic competitiveness, national security, or the privacy or security of human genetic information, to enhance long-term innovation and competitiveness in engineering biology in the United States.

Develop a plan to utilize Federal programs, such as the Small Business Innovation Research Program and the Small Business Technology Transfer Program (as described in ), in support of the activities described in .section 638 of title 15section 19132(b)(3) of this title

In carrying out this section, take into consideration the recommendations of the advisory committee established under , the results of the workshop convened under , existing reports on related topics, and the views of academic, State, industry, and other appropriate groups.section 19134 of this titlesection 19132 of this title

A summarized agency budget in support of the Initiative for the current fiscal year, including a breakout of spending for each agency participating in the Program, and for the development and acquisition of any research facilities and instrumentation.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the amount and number of awards made under the Small Business Innovation Research Program and the Small Business Technology Transfer Program (as described in ) in support of the Initiative.section 638 of title 15

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the amount and number of projects funded under joint solicitations by a collaboration of not fewer than two agencies participating in the Initiative.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of effects of newly-funded projects by the Initiative.

children.label: A
children.text: provide technical and administrative support to the interagency committee and the advisory committee established under subsection (a) and ;section 19134 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: serve as the point of contact on Federal engineering biology activities for government organizations, academia, industry, professional societies, State governments, interested citizen groups, and others to exchange technical and programmatic information;

children.label: C
children.text: oversee interagency coordination of the Initiative, including by encouraging and supporting joint agency solicitation and selection of applications for funding of activities under the Initiative, as appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: conduct public outreach, including dissemination of findings and recommendations of the advisory committee, as appropriate;

children.label: E
children.text: serve as the coordinator of ethical, legal, environmental, safety, security, and other appropriate societal input; and

children.label: F
children.text: promote access to, and early application of, the technologies, innovations, and expertise derived from Initiative activities to agency missions and systems across the Federal Government, and to United States industry, including startup companies.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 2 Funding
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy, in coordination with each participating Federal department and agency, as appropriate, shall develop and annually update an estimate of the funds necessary to carry out the activities of the Initiative Coordination Office and submit such estimate with an agreed summary of contributions from each agency to Congress as part of the President’s annual budget request to Congress.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 3 Termination
children.text: The Initiative Coordination Office established under this subsection shall terminate on the date that is 10 years after .August 9, 2022

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: d Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to alter the policies, processes, or practices of individual Federal agencies in effect on the day before , relating to the conduct of biomedical research and advanced development, including the solicitation and review of extramural research proposals.August 9, 2022
